headline: LoL's EWC Playoffs Are Heating Up And Not Just Because of Paris in July
dek: With only eight teams left, the League of Legends tournament at the Esports World Cup 2026 enters a single-elimination bracket, promising intense competition and significant prize money after its recent relocation.
body_markdown:
The battle for supremacy in the Esports World Cup 2026's League of Legends tournament has entered its most thrilling phase: the playoffs. With the group stage now complete and eight teams eliminated, a mere eight squads remain, poised to clash in a high-stakes, single-elimination knockout bracket. The action kicked off on July 17, pushing competitors closer to the ultimate prize.

This League of Legends showdown is a key attraction within the broader Esports World Cup, a colossal event currently unfolding in Paris, France. The tournament series began on July 6 and runs all the way through August 23, 2026. It's a truly massive undertaking, featuring 25 separate tournaments across 24 different esports titles. The collective prize pool stands at an impressive $75 million, with an additional $30 million allocated for the Club Championship, making it one of the most lucrative events in competitive gaming history.

League of Legends is undoubtedly one of July's marquee attractions, running its specific tournament from July 13 to July 19. The intensity of these matches is amplified by the single-elimination format, meaning every game is a fight for survival. One misstep, and a team's journey is over.

The 2026 Esports World Cup's current location in Paris marks a significant change for the event. Originally slated for Saudi Arabia, the tournament was relocated due to security concerns stemming from the 2026 Iran war, as reported by Wikipedia. This European backdrop now hosts the intense competition.

As the playoffs unfold, every play, every objective, and every team fight is under the microscope. Providing crucial insights into these high-pressure moments is Stream Hatchet, which is delivering real-time analytics and performance metrics as an official data partner for the EWC 2026. With the grand finale approaching, the remaining teams are battling not just for glory, but for a piece of one of the largest prize pools in esports history.
